2604.19301.title: Large Language Models Exhibit Normative Conformity
2604.19301.paper_summary: The conformity bias exhibited by large language models (LLMs) can pose a significant challenge to decision-making in LLM-based multi-agent systems (LLM-MAS). While many prior studies have treated "conformity" simply as a matter of opinion change, this study introduces the social psychological distinction between informational conformity and normative conformity in order to understand LLM conformity at the mechanism level. Specifically, we design new tasks to distinguish between informational conformity, in which participants in a discussion are motivated to make accurate judgments, and normative conformity, in which participants are motivated to avoid conflict or gain acceptance within a group. We then conduct experiments based on these task settings. The experimental results show that, among the six LLMs evaluated, up to five exhibited tendencies toward not only informational conformity but also normative conformity. Furthermore, intriguingly, we demonstrate that by manipulating subtle aspects of the social context, it may be possible to control the target toward which a particular LLM directs its normative conformity. These findings suggest that decision-making in LLM-MAS may be vulnerable to manipulation by a small number of malicious users. In addition, through analysis of internal vectors associated with informational and normative conformity, we suggest that although both behaviors appear externally as the same form of "conformity," they may in fact be driven by distinct internal mechanisms. Taken together, these results may serve as an initial milestone toward understanding how "norms" are implemented in LLMs and how they influence group dynamics.
2604.19301.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）所表現出的從眾偏見可能對基於LLM的多智能體系統（LLM-MAS）中的決策過程構成重大挑戰。雖然許多先前的研究將「從眾」簡單地視為意見變化的問題，但本研究引入了社會心理學中信息性從眾和規範性從眾之間的區別，以便在機制層面理解LLM的從眾行為。具體而言，我們設計了新的任務來區分信息性從眾，即討論中的參與者被激勵做出準確判斷，以及規範性從眾，即參與者被激勵避免衝突或在群體中獲得接受。我們隨後根據這些任務設置進行實驗。實驗結果顯示，在六個被評估的LLM中，最多有五個顯示出不僅是信息性從眾，還有規範性從眾的傾向。此外，有趣的是，我們證明通過操控社會情境中的微妙方面，可能可以控制特定LLM所指向的規範性從眾的目標。這些發現表明，LLM-MAS中的決策過程可能容易受到少數惡意用戶的操控。此外，通過分析與信息性和規範性從眾相關的內部向量，我們建議，儘管這兩種行為在外部看起來是相同形式的「從眾」，但實際上可能由不同的內部機制驅動。綜合來看，這些結果可能為理解「規範」如何在LLM中實施以及它們如何影響群體動態提供了一個初步的里程碑。
2604.19301.author: Mikako Bito et.al.
2604.19301.authors: Mikako Bito, Keita Nishimoto, Kimitaka Asatani, Ichiro Sakata
